campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall budget status. They also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, like adding biking, walking, and transit-friendly improvements to the city.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
New multimodal transportation plan (bikes, pedestrians, public transit improvements)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipped the typical first reading step to speed up the process. The ordinance is scheduled to be formally adopted at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing conducted to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
Council set to vote on final passage at next regular meeting
First reading requirement waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from March and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting covered routine administrative items including approving records from multiple March meetings and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six March 2026 meetings
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given approval to execute contract

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review how well the city is serving kids and teens, including updates on youth programs, summer safety initiatives, and support services. They also heard reports on code enforcement activities and library facilities to understand how these services are performing and where improvements are needed.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and summer programs for teens
Youth intervention and support services performance review
Code enforcement activities and violations in San José
Library facility improvements and customer experience upgrades

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate both in person and online via Zoom. Residents could join the meeting video call or by phone to speak during public comment periods on agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom for residents unable to attend in person
Public comment procedures for both online and phone participants
Instructions for raising hand to speak during agenda items

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held their regular meeting on April 9, 2026, starting at 7 P.M. with a flag salute and personal stories shared by three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group approved the official meeting minutes from their March 12 gathering and moved forward with their regular business items.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission monthly meeting
Personal stories from Commissioners 5, 6, and 7 about Sunnyvale
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Regular public hearings and general business items

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community initiatives including a food-focused program called Budget and Bites, hear from a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and learn about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved meeting minutes from their previous March session and opened the floor for residents to comment on any city matters.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City of Cupertino summer jobs for youth
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using both in-person and online options so residents can participate however works best for them. The city provided multiple ways for the public to join remotely through Zoom or by phone, and residents could submit written comments about agenda items before the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and remote via Zoom)
Public can call in by phone at 1(669) 900-6833
Written public comments accepted through eComment system on City Council Agenda page
Meeting accommodates both in-person and remote participation

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve several administrative items and consider updates to the city's long-term housing and community development plans for 2025-2030. The council also discussed potential changes to how council vacancies are filled and reviewed upcoming meeting schedules and agendas.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's 5-year housing and community development plan
Changes to how the city council fills vacant seats
Council member requests for new work items and projects
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling for future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss and approve the city's gas utility budget and rates for the next fiscal year (2027). The council reviewed financial forecasts, approved moving money between accounts, and adjusted gas rates for residential and commercial customers in three different service categories.
keyTopics: Gas utility rates increasing for residential and commercial customers
City transferring reserves and general fund money to support gas utility operations
Approval of fiscal year 2027 budget for natural gas service
Rate changes for three customer categories: regular homes (G-1), multi-unit buildings (G-2), and large businesses (G-3)
